Politics.headline: Trump Picks Off Massie in Kentucky
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump got his revenge on Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie, who lost to Trump-endorsed former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein in Kentucky's 4th District on Tuesday. It's the latest in a string of primary victories for the president that cements his grip on the GOP.

Politics.headline: Washington: Trump's Special Tax Status
Politics.detail: The Justice Department has granted President Trump, his family, and businesses immunity from ongoing inquiries into their taxes, a potentially lucrative arrangement that could shield the president from significant financial liability. The provision protects the president, his relatives, and his businesses from pending audits and tax prosecutions.

Politics.headline: Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers for First Time
Politics.detail: Four Republicans voted with nearly all Democrats to advance the Iran war powers measure, with GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy joining the Republican defectors for the first time. This resolution seeks to limit President Trump's ability to wage war against Iran without congressional approval.

Politics.headline: Xi and Putin unite in criticism of U.S. on nuclear and security issues
Politics.detail: China and Russia condemned U.S. President Donald Trump's missile defense shield plans and Washington's “irresponsible” nuclear policy at a joint summit. Xi and Putin said that Trump's plan for a ground- and space-based missile interceptor system posed a threat to global strategic stability.

Politics.headline: I'm the Foreign Minister of Sweden. Don't Overestimate Russia.
Politics.detail: Maria Malmer Stenergard, the Minister for Foreign Affairs of Sweden, published an opinion piece in The New York Times on May 20, 2026, regarding the approach to Russia. The article suggests not overestimating Russia's capabilities and influence.

Finance & Economy.headline: UN lowers forecast for global economic growth in 2026 over Mideast energy crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: The United Nations has lowered its forecast for global economic growth and raised the prospects for inflation in response to Middle East crises and rising oil prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's infrastructure output grows 1.7% in April; cement, steel, electricity lift core sectors
Finance & Economy.detail: India's infrastructure output experienced growth in April, with key sectors like cement, steel, and electricity contributing to the increase.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK inflation slows to 2.8% as energy price cap softens impact of rising fuel costs
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK inflation rate eased to 2.8%, primarily due to a softening impact from the energy price cap on rising fuel costs, providing some relief.

Finance & Economy.headline: MAS Revokes the Major Payment Institution Licence of Bsquared Technology Pte Ltd
Finance & Economy.detail: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has revoked the Major Payment Institution Licence of Bsquared Technology Pte Ltd.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Stocks Inch Higher Ahead of Nvidia
Finance & Economy.detail: US equities were mostly higher on Wednesday ahead of Nvidia results after the bell, due to update the state of the AI economy that has supported the stock market this year.

Technology.headline: Thales Announces Strategic Partnership with Google Cloud to Launch a New Sovereign Cloud in Germany
Technology.detail: Thales and Google Cloud have partnered to launch a new European sovereign cloud offering in Germany, providing advanced cloud capabilities while ensuring data confidentiality, security, and sovereignty.

Technology.headline: Tech launches today (May 20, 2026): Slimmest fire stick, Canva in Gemini, and more
Technology.detail: Amazon, Canva and COLORFUL unveiled a range of new products and integrations today, including a redesigned Fire TV Stick HD, Canva's Gemini integration and new AMD Ryzen-focused motherboards.

Technology.headline: Trane Technologies Opens Global AI Lab and Showroom, Accelerating the Future of Autonomous Buildings
Technology.detail: Trane Technologies has opened the BrainBox AI Trane Technologies AI Lab and showroom in Montréal to accelerate the development of AI-driven solutions that reduce energy consumption and carbon emissions in buildings.

Technology.headline: ATOSS Opens AI Technology Hub in Bengaluru to Drive AI-Powered Workforce Management Solutions
Technology.detail: ATOSS Software SE is expanding its AI capabilities by opening a new technology hub in Bengaluru, India, to focus on AI-driven product innovation for workforce management solutions.

Technology.headline: Kyndryl and DPD expand partnership to modernize mission-critical environments in Portugal
Technology.detail: Kyndryl and DPD announced an expanded partnership to modernize DPD's mission-critical environments in Portugal through managed services for hybrid technology environments.

Health.headline: WHO concerned about 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak, with 131 now dead
Health.detail: The World Health Organization has expressed concern over the increasing scale and speed of the Ebola outbreak in Congo, where 131 people have died.

Health.headline: Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly honours global champions advancing primary health care
Health.detail: The Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly recognized global leaders for their contributions to advancing primary health care.

Health.headline: What to know about the Bundibugyo virus, a species of Ebola causing an outbreak in Congo
Health.detail: The Ebola outbreak in Congo is caused by the Bundibugyo virus, which is less common and lacks specific treatments or vaccines, complicating the response. More than 130 people are suspected to have died from the outbreak.

Health.headline: Medical Innovation: Mayo Clinic and Bayesian Health co-develop new AI-powered solution to expand palliative care access and improve patient outcomes
Health.detail: Mayo Clinic and Bayesian Health have jointly developed a new AI solution designed to increase access to palliative care and enhance patient outcomes.

Health.headline: Antidepressants in Pregnancy: New Data on Autism, ADHD Risk
Health.detail: New data has emerged regarding the potential risks of autism and ADHD associated with antidepressant use during pregnancy.

Sports.headline: Knicks Rally From 22 Down to Beat Cavaliers in OT
Sports.detail: Jalen Brunson led the Knicks back from a 22-point deficit in the 4th quarter for a 115-104 win over the Cavaliers in Game 1 of the East Finals in the NBA playoffs. Brunson's performance was crucial as New York completed a stunning comeback in overtime.

Sports.headline: Jason Kidd Out as Mavericks Coach After Five Seasons
Sports.detail: Jason Kidd is no longer the coach of the Dallas Mavericks after five seasons with the team. The announcement comes two weeks after the hiring of team president Masai Ujiri.

Sports.headline: Arsenal on verge of Premier League title after beating Burnley
Sports.detail: Arsenal is close to securing the Premier League title after a victory over Burnley. The win puts them in a favorable position to claim the championship.

Sports.headline: Coco Gauff has a new mindset for title defense and more confidence on her serve for FRENCH OPEN '26
Sports.detail: Coco Gauff is entering the French Open '26 with a refreshed mindset as she defends her title. She also expressed increased confidence in her serve.

Sports.headline: Erling Haaland and Pep Guardiola disagree as 'angry' Arsenal message sent
Sports.detail: Erling Haaland and Pep Guardiola are reportedly in disagreement amidst an 'angry' message sent by Arsenal. The specific details of the disagreement and message remain unclear.

Entertainment.headline: Movie Review: 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' makes a clumsy big-screen debut
Entertainment.detail: “The Mandalorian and Grogu” hits theaters nearly seven years after the last “Star Wars” movie, receiving a rating of ★★★★.

Entertainment.headline: Leon Thomas to receive ASCAP Vanguard Award for shaping the future of R&B
Entertainment.detail: Leon Thomas will be honored with the ASCAP Vanguard Award for his contributions and influence in shaping the future of R&B music.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift Snaps Selfies as Travis Kelce's Date at George Karlaftis' Wedding
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Swift was seen taking selfies while attending George Karlaftis' wedding with Travis Kelce.

Entertainment.headline: Beanie Feldstein on takeaways from kids' book
Entertainment.detail: Actor Beanie Feldstein discusses the theme of her debut children's book, which focuses on the power of friendship, and becoming a parent soon.

Entertainment.headline: Hayden Panettiere Says Oscar Winner Showed Her His Testicles
Entertainment.detail: Hayden Panettiere made a claim that an Oscar winner exposed his testicles to her.

Crime & Justice.headline: Prisoner escapes from NYPD custody outside Brooklyn courthouse
Crime & Justice.detail: The NYPD is searching for a prisoner that bolted from police custody in Brooklyn Tuesday afternoon.

Crime & Justice.headline: Knife robberies down by more than a fifth
Crime & Justice.detail: Knife robberies have fallen sharply in major cities thanks to joint government and police action to tackle one of the most harmful forms of violence and make communities safe; new data shows robberies involving a knife in the 7 highest volume areas are now more than a fifth lower (21%) than they were in June 2024.

Crime & Justice.headline: Luigi Mangione Appears In Court On NY State Murder Charges
Crime & Justice.detail: Mangione supporters should not have been given press passes, mayor says; Luigi Mangione supporters have loudly made their feelings known outside every court appearance, but several are now in court with official press passes.

Crime & Justice.headline: Paul Caneiro gets life in prison for killing brother, sister-in-law, their children
Crime & Justice.detail: A jury found Paul Caneiro guilty in February of murdering his brother, sister-in-law, niece and nephew before setting their New Jersey mansion on fire in 2018.

Crime & Justice.headline: PM says police should prioritise serious crime after Linehan arrest
Crime & Justice.detail: Following the arrest of Graham Linehan, the Prime Minister stated that police should prioritize serious crime.

Education.headline: Liberia: School Fees Block Children's Access to Education
Education.detail: Human Rights Watch reports that mandatory fees in Liberian public schools, despite a legal guarantee of free education for grades 1 to 9, place a heavy financial burden on families, forcing many children to delay enrollment, miss school, or drop out altogether. The report urges the Liberian government to eliminate registration fees, expand school grants, and improve education quality.

Education.headline: Integrated Education Support Programme launched
Education.detail: The Integrated Education Support Programme in Northern Ireland is designed to ensure schools have comprehensive support, engage with staff, parents, pupils, and the wider community, and meet the statutory requirements for Integrated Education under the Integrated Education Act 2022. It supports schools in implementing cultural and curricular changes and building a case for change based on demand, sustainability, and readiness.

Education.headline: NC joins lawsuit against US Department of Education over health care student loan changes
Education.detail: North Carolina's Attorney General Jeff Jackson has joined a lawsuit with 24 other states and the District of Columbia against the U.S. Department of Education, arguing that a new rule limiting the amount students can borrow for health care programs is illegal and could worsen the state's nursing shortage. The rule change caps federal loans for graduate nursing students at $20,500 a year, up to a total of $100,000, which Jackson argues is insufficient given the length and cost of these advanced degree programs.

Education.headline: Teach First wins flagship teacher training contract
Education.detail: Teach First has been awarded a contract for the High Potential Initial Teacher Training programme, which is a key component of the government's plan to recruit 6,500 more teachers by the end of the current Parliament. This program aims to support teacher training and recruitment efforts in the UK.

Education.headline: DfE Update academies: 20 May 2026
Education.detail: The Department for Education (DfE) has announced new arrangements for establishing state-funded schools starting September 1, 2026, following Royal Assent of the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Act 2026, including updated guidance on opening new schools. Additionally, free school meals eligibility is being expanded to all children from households receiving Universal Credit, with information webinars scheduled in June to discuss the updated FSM guidance for schools and local authorities.

Lifestyle.headline: Why 'non-ultra-processed' food labels won't make Americans eat better
Lifestyle.detail: Food manufacturers want us to buy more food, and new labels won't change their motives or our habits.

Lifestyle.headline: A 6-year-old and his goats raised over $15,000 for pet shelters after his dog died
Lifestyle.detail: A 6-year-old invited strangers to feed his goats as a way of raising money for pet shelters after his dog passed away.

Lifestyle.headline: The lifestyle changes that could add an extra five years to your life
Lifestyle.detail: Healthy living could prolong the lives of some by five years.

Lifestyle.headline: And, lo! The latest fad diet is upon us – and it's called 'eating biblically'
Lifestyle.detail: Some Christian conservatives are only eating foods mentioned in the Bible.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside India's 275-Year-Old Colosseum In Assam, Rebuilt From Ruins
Lifestyle.detail: A historical colosseum in Assam, India, dating back 275 years, has been rebuilt from ruins.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The balance of global power is shifting fast, but Britain is stuck in the same old Brexit rut
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Britain's focus on Brexit continues to hinder its ability to adapt to the rapidly changing global power dynamics.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The die isn't cast: France is pessimistic, but not doomed to far-right rule
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite the current pessimism in France, the country is not necessarily destined to be ruled by the far-right.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on saving for old age: alarming shortfalls set the scene for a pensions overhaul
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Significant shortfalls in savings for old age are creating a need for a comprehensive overhaul of pension systems.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on domestic workers: Indonesia shows that, against the odds, they are fighting for their rights
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Indonesia's efforts demonstrate that domestic workers are making progress in their fight for rights, despite the challenges they face.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The AI agent is learning workers' rights.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An AI agent is learning about the rights of workers.

Zimbabwe.headline: President to lead Culture festivities in Byo
Zimbabwe.detail: President is expected to lead Culture festivities in Bulawayo.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Economy stable despite external shocks'
Zimbabwe.detail: The economy is reportedly stable despite external shocks.

Zimbabwe.headline: Amendment Bill: Parliament collates verdict . . . sifts through submissions
Zimbabwe.detail: Parliament is collating the verdict and sifting through submissions regarding the Amendment Bill.

Zimbabwe.headline: Another wheat milestone beckons. . . Arda targets 325 000 tonnes
Zimbabwe.detail: Another wheat milestone is expected as Arda targets 325,000 tonnes.

Zimbabwe.headline: '4th Industrial Revolution shouldn't deepen inequalities'
Zimbabwe.detail: It is being discussed that the 4th Industrial Revolution shouldn't deepen inequalities.
